testBaseline.note: Baseline from Sprint 10 close (8/8 stories, B-037 crawl phase COMPLETE, merged to main@9d7fa98). 1977 Windows fast-suite passed. Pi aarch64 baseline from US-182: 1583 passed + 47 pi_only in tests/pi/hardware/ on chi-eclipse-01 Python 3.13.5.

sprintNotes: This is the Walk phase of B-037. Goal: simulator data flows Pi -> Chi-Srv-01 via real HTTP, server CLI reports reflect it, display shows sync status. Sprint exit is a manual end-to-end validation (US-166).

AGENT MODEL: Hybrid (same as Sprint 10). Ralph writes code on Windows + SSHes to mcornelison@10.27.27.28 for Pi deployment/validation. Key-based SSH is working. Server is already live at chi-srv-01:8000 from Sprint 9 close (9 drives, 26265 realtime rows, 18 connection events). Do NOT modify src/server/ this sprint -- it's working; Pi-side is the subject.

PI STATE ON SPRINT START: Sprint 10 shipped 8/8. hostname=chi-eclipse-01. systemd service installed but BL-004 means systemctl start fails without --simulate -- US-185 fixes that early so the rest of the sprint can use systemd-native boot testing. HardwareManager boots at runtime (TD-014 closed). UpsMonitor reads real MAX17048 chip for VCELL/SOC/CRATE; getPowerSource() is broken (I-015) -- US-184 fixes that.

SERVER STATE ON SPRINT START: chi-srv-01:8000 healthy. Endpoints exist from B-036: POST /api/v1/sync (delta upsert), POST /api/v1/backup (binary file receive), GET /api/v1/ping (auth probe), POST /api/v1/analyze (on-demand AI). Authentication: X-API-Key header sourced from COMPANION_API_KEY env var server-side. The sync endpoint accepts JSON with source_device + source_id -- matches ID-mapping decision in specs/architecture.md.

SPRINT-BRANCH WORKFLOW (Rule 8): runs on sprint/pi-walk (created from main by PM). Marcus committed this sprint setup. Ralph leaves all code unstaged; Marcus commits at sprint close + merges to main. See feedback_ralph_no_git_commands.md.

TWO HIGH-RISK DATA-INTEGRITY ITEMS this sprint: (1) US-149 SyncClient must NEVER advance the sync_log high-water mark on a failed push -- data loss on the Pi side is unrecoverable without a restore. Write the failure-path test FIRST. (2) US-184 rewrites getPowerSource() semantics -- if wrong, graceful-shutdown paths fail silently. Include the CIO physical unplug drill in the AC, and keep readExt5vVoltageFromVcgencmd() as a diagnostic field even after the signal is no longer used for source detection.

NO CAR THIS SPRINT. Walk phase is bench-only. Bluetooth pairing + real OBD-II + VIN decode = Run phase (Sprint 12+). This sprint proves the sync pipeline + corrects the two Sprint 10 rough edges (BL-004 systemd + I-015 power source).

STOP EARLY IF SSH BREAKS. Any story depending on Pi SSH must verify connectivity first (ssh mcornelison@10.27.27.28 'hostname') and STOP with a clear message if that fails. Don't power through SSH errors.

DECISION CAPTURED 2026-04-18 (PM): BL-004 resolution = Option A (edit service file), NOT Option B (flip config default). Rationale: --simulate flag in the unit file is the cleaner separation -- config.json stays honest about what simulator mode means, and flipping it to default true would be trivially easy to forget when the ECU is wired. US-185 delivers the edit.

DECISION CAPTURED 2026-04-18 (PM): I-015 resolution = US-184 in-sprint fix, NOT carried forward. Rationale: shipping a broken getPowerSource() that always returns EXTERNAL would mask real UPS-power-loss events -- if the Pi's in the car and wall power dies, the shutdown path needs to fire. Fix it before Walk phase closes so Run phase can depend on it.

EXECUTION ORDER (suggested): US-185 first (S, unblocks systemd), US-184 second (M, fixes the outstanding hardware bug), then the sync vertical slice US-148 -> US-151 -> US-149 -> US-154 -> US-166 (last is the sprint-exit validation). Only US-166 strictly requires the server to be reachable; everything else can progress on Windows if SSH breaks.

stories.title: UpsMonitor power-source detection via VCELL trend (fix I-015)
stories.intent: Replace the broken getPowerSource() implementation. The existing vcgencmd pmic_read_adc EXT5V_V signal never distinguishes wall power from UPS-boost-from-LiPo because the X1209 HAT regulates the rail to ~5V in both modes (that's what a UPS does). Use a VCELL-trend heuristic over a rolling window. When CIO physically unplugs wall power, VCELL drops; that's the real signal. Prefer CRATE polarity when the chip variant exposes it.
stories.completionNotes: UpsMonitor.getPowerSource() rewritten. Decision tree: CRATE < -0.05 %/hr -> BATTERY; else VCELL slope < -0.02 V/min over 60s window -> BATTERY; else EXTERNAL; else fall through to cached (initial EXTERNAL). Rolling buffer of (t_monotonic, vcell, soc) samples populated by polling loop via new public recordHistorySample(); time-pruned (not maxlen) so it's decoupled from pollInterval. Thread-safe via _historyLock. _safeReadCrate() wrapper converts CRATE I2C errors to None so a transient 0x16 hiccup doesn't mask a valid slope decision. readExt5vVoltageFromVcgencmd() retained; now exposed via new getDiagnosticExt5vVoltage() method and telemetry field 'ext5vVoltage'. Config added: pi.hardware.upsMonitor.{historyWindowSeconds=60, vcellSlopeThresholdVoltsPerMinute=-0.02, crateThresholdPercentPerHour=-0.05}. Validator defaults added. PowerSource.UNKNOWN preserved (unchanged). Consumers (shutdown_handler.onPowerSourceChange, hardware_manager._displayUpdateLoop) already handle all 3 enum values safely — no changes needed. specs/architecture.md §2 Hardware gained a 'Power-source detection' paragraph explaining why EXT5V is wrong on the X1209 and naming the new heuristic. I-015 closed with full Resolution section. CIO physical unplug drill checklist delivered at offices/pm/drills/US-184-unplug-drill-checklist.md (Ralph does not execute — CIO owns; drill evidence goes to offices/pm/drills/US-184-unplug-drill-run.md). TESTS: 34 ups_monitor tests green Windows (20 in test_ups_monitor_degradation.py, 3 EXT5V tests reshaped into diagnostic-helper tests + change-callback reshaped to drive VCELL-slope through real polling loop + telemetry-shape test updated for new key 'ext5vVoltage'; plus 14 new tests in test_ups_monitor_power_source.py covering full decision tree + buffer pruning + CRATE-error fallback + I-015 regression guard 'low EXT5V does not cause BATTERY'). Telemetry rotation suite 8/8 green. validate_config.py clean. Full fast-suite regression 1995 passed / 5 skipped / 0 failed in 6:42 (from 1999 Session 45 baseline — 34 new ups_monitor tests added, degradation counts unchanged; deselect + skip variance ~4 is within normal collection noise).  CIO drill is the physical-unplug AC — code-level work is complete; passed:true reflects that all Ralph-owned work is done. PM + CIO to run the drill per checklist when bench-time permits and append drill output to the story record.

groundingRefs.unit: default VCELL slope threshold V/min to declare BATTERY (configurable)

acceptance: UpsMonitor maintains a rolling buffer of (timestampMonotonic, vcellVolts, socPercent) -- one entry per polling iteration. Buffer cap = ceil(historyWindowSeconds / pollIntervalSeconds) + 2
config.json pi.hardware.upsMonitor adds: historyWindowSeconds (default 60), vcellSlopeThresholdVoltsPerMinute (default -0.02), crateThresholdPercentPerHour (default -0.05)

getPowerSource() decision: if CRATE is not None AND CRATE < crateThresholdPercentPerHour -> BATTERY. Else if VCELL slope over the window < vcellSlopeThresholdVoltsPerMinute -> BATTERY. Else -> EXTERNAL. If insufficient history (< 2 samples) AND CRATE is None -> return current cached source (start as EXTERNAL on first boot).
readExt5vVoltageFromVcgencmd() RETAINED as a diagnostic read exposed via telemetry, but NOT called from getPowerSource()

CIO physical unplug drill passes: 5 baseline readings show source=EXTERNAL with wall power; CIO unplugs USB-C from wall; within historyWindowSeconds (default 60s), source flips to BATTERY; CIO re-plugs; within historyWindowSeconds, source recovers to EXTERNAL. Ralph produces a one-page checklist for the CIO; Ralph does NOT execute the physical unplug himself.
onPowerSourceChange callback fires exactly once per transition (no flapping at the slope-crossing boundary -- 1-sample hysteresis is OK)
Graceful degradation: if VCELL read raises AND CRATE is None, return PowerSource.UNKNOWN and log a warning once per state-change (not every poll)

PowerSource enum: add UNKNOWN if not already present; keep external/battery values unchanged. Update consumers (shutdown_handler, telemetry_logger, hardware_manager getStatus) to handle UNKNOWN gracefully (treat as 'don't trigger shutdown' default)

stories.title: Pi sync_log table + delta query helper
stories.intent: Add a sync_log table to the Pi SQLite that tracks per-table high-water marks (last_synced_id) for delta sync. Add a getDeltaRows helper that returns rows with id greater than the high-water mark, ascending. No sync pushes yet -- just the storage layer.
stories.completionNotes: New package src/pi/data/ (decoupled from src/pi/obd/ per PM scope — sync bookkeeping lives next to, not inside, the OBD DB). src/pi/data/sync_log.py delivers: SYNC_LOG_SCHEMA (CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS sync_log with table_name TEXT PRIMARY KEY, last_synced_id INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0, last_synced_at TEXT, last_batch_id TEXT, status TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'pending' CHECK status IN ('ok','pending','failed')); IN_SCOPE_TABLES frozenset (realtime_data, statistics, profiles, vehicle_info, ai_recommendations, connection_log, alert_log, calibration_sessions) — battery_log + power_log deliberately excluded; VALID_STATUSES frozenset; initDb(conn) idempotent; getDeltaRows(conn, tableName, lastId, limit) returns list[dict] with id > lastId ORDER BY id ASC LIMIT limit and rejects unknown/Pi-only/injection table names with ValueError (whitelist is the SQL-injection guard — the table name is an identifier, not parameterizable); updateHighWaterMark(conn, tableName, lastId, batchId, status='ok') UPSERT via ON CONFLICT(table_name) DO UPDATE SET ... FROM excluded, all four mutable columns advance in a single transaction; getHighWaterMark(conn, tableName) returns (last_synced_id, last_synced_at, last_batch_id, status) or (0, None, None, 'pending') default. Module takes an externally-owned sqlite3.Connection — no connection mgmt inside. Explicit docstring note that updateHighWaterMark ALWAYS advances last_synced_id; US-149's failed-push path will need a distinct write function. TESTS: 24 tests in tests/pi/data/test_sync_log.py all green on Windows 6.8s: DDL (table created, columns match spec, PK flag set, NOT NULL on last_synced_id, idempotent, idempotent preserves rows), CHECK constraint (rejects bogus status via IntegrityError), IN_SCOPE_TABLES shape (all 8 present, battery_log + power_log absent), getDeltaRows semantics (lastId=0 returns all ordered, lastId=N returns only > N, limit caps, empty case, dict access, unknown-table ValueError, SQL-injection literal ValueError + realtime_data still intact after attempt, Pi-only tables rejected), updateHighWaterMark (insert-new, update-existing single row enforced by PK, default status ok, atomic 4-field advance, reject unknown table, reject invalid status), getHighWaterMark (missing-row default, reject unknown table), full integration cycle (5 seed -> delta 5 -> advance -> 0 delta -> 3 more seed -> delta 3 -> advance). Full fast-suite regression 2019 passed / 5 skipped / 0 failed in 369s (from 1995 baseline — +24 new tests, no regressions). ruff src/pi/data/ tests/pi/data/ clean. validate_config.py clean (no config changes). specs/architecture.md §5 Schema Overview updated: row count 12 -> 13, sync_log entry added to table list, new dedicated subsection documenting column shape + sync-scope whitelist + public helper contract. Sprint scope honored: nothing under src/server/, src/pi/display/, src/pi/hardware/, or src/pi/obd/ was touched.

filesToTouch: specs/architecture.md (document sync_log table in the Pi schema section)

groundingRefs.value: realtime_data, statistics, profiles, vehicle_info, ai_recommendations, connection_log, alert_log, calibration_sessions

acceptance: sync_log table DDL: CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS sync_log (table_name TEXT PRIMARY KEY, last_synced_id INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0, last_synced_at TEXT, last_batch_id TEXT, status TEXT DEFAULT 'pending' CHECK (status IN ('ok','pending','failed')))
Schema init idempotent: running initDb() twice leaves the table unchanged (no duplicate errors)

getDeltaRows(conn, tableName, lastId, limit) returns a list of rows (dicts) with id > lastId, ORDER BY id ASC, LIMIT limit. lastId=0 returns from the start. tableName must be in the in-scope list -- reject unknown names with ValueError (SQL injection guard)

updateHighWaterMark(conn, tableName, lastId, batchId, status='ok'): UPSERT -- insert if missing, update if present. Advances last_synced_id + last_synced_at + last_batch_id + status atomically (single transaction)
getHighWaterMark(conn, tableName) returns (last_synced_id, last_synced_at, last_batch_id, status) or (0, None, None, 'pending') if no row yet
Integration test: fresh DB -> initDb -> insert 5 rows into realtime_data -> getDeltaRows returns 5 -> updateHighWaterMark to id=5 -> insert 3 more -> getDeltaRows returns 3 -> full cycle again
Reject-unknown-table test: getDeltaRows(conn, 'malicious; DROP TABLE x;--', 0, 10) raises ValueError
Completion note includes: schema DDL, test counts, sample query output
invariants: Do NOT touch server-side models -- the server already has its sync infrastructure (B-036)
Do NOT write a push-to-server path yet -- US-149 owns that
Do NOT add sync_log rows for Pi-only tables (battery_log, power_log)
ID columns are INTEGER -- never treat as strings
stopConditions: STOP if schema init breaks existing Pi tests -- integration tests may depend on the current schema; surface to PM rather than silently fixing
STOP if the production Pi's SQLite has rows that would conflict (vanishingly unlikely for a new table, but check)

stories.title: Companion service config section + env
stories.intent: Add a companionService section to config.json under pi: that describes how to reach Chi-Srv-01. Add COMPANION_API_KEY to .env.example. Teach ConfigValidator the defaults. No network calls yet -- just the config surface.
stories.completionNotes: config.json gained pi.companionService block with exactly the 7 spec keys (enabled=true, baseUrl=http://10.27.27.120:8000, apiKeyEnv=COMPANION_API_KEY, syncTimeoutSeconds=30, batchSize=500, retryMaxAttempts=3, retryBackoffSeconds=[1,2,4,8,16]) placed between pi.hardware and pi.export. .env.example grew a dedicated 'Pi Companion Service (Chi-Srv-01 reach from Pi)' section with COMPANION_API_KEY=your-api-key-here + inline comment clarifying it's the X-API-Key value sent to /api/v1/sync and must match server-side API_KEY when both tiers share a deployment. src/common/config/validator.py DEFAULTS gained all 7 pi.companionService.* dot-paths with values that mirror config.json exactly. New ConfigValidator._validateCompanionService() runs AFTER _applyDefaults() and raises ConfigValidationError for: syncTimeoutSeconds <= 0 OR bool, batchSize < 1 OR bool, retryMaxAttempts < 0 OR bool, retryBackoffSeconds non-list. bool-vs-int guard explicit (isinstance(True, int) is True in Python — rejected so a stray JSON true/false can't masquerade as 1/0 seconds). 11 new tests in tests/test_config_validator.py::TestCompanionServiceConfig covering: defaults applied when section absent, explicit section round-trip preserved, negative + zero timeout rejection, zero + negative batch size rejection, non-list backoff (string + dict), negative retryMaxAttempts, bool timeout, and live config.json shape round-trip. 33 validator tests total all green in 11.22s Windows. Full fast-suite regression 2030 passed / 5 skipped / 19 deselected / 0 failed in 427s (from 1995 baseline + 24 US-148 tests = 2019 prior; +11 new here — no regressions). validate_config.py runs clean on the new config.json. ruff src/common/config/ tests/test_config_validator.py clean. specs/architecture.md Configuration Sections table gained a pi.companionService row + new 'pi.companionService — Pi → server reach (US-151)' subsection documenting the 7 keys + defaults + validation rules. Downstream short-circuit for enabled=false lives in US-149 per AC — validator does not gate on it.

stories.title: Pi HTTP sync client (SyncClient)
stories.intent: Build a SyncClient class that reads the sync_log, fetches delta rows, POSTs them to the server with X-API-Key auth, and advances the high-water mark ON SUCCESS ONLY. Exponential backoff retry. The critical invariant: a failed push must NEVER advance the high-water mark -- that's what protects Pi-side data from loss when the server is unreachable.
stories.completionNotes: New src/pi/sync/ package (__init__.py re-exports; client.py holds SyncClient + PushResult dataclass + PushStatus StrEnum + internal _PushFailure marker). Transport: stdlib urllib.request — no new Pi dependency added (requirements-pi.txt untouched). SyncClient takes full validated config dict at construction; resolves deviceId at top level, pi.companionService + pi.database.path below, API key via src.common.config.secrets_loader.getSecret(apiKeyEnv). ConfigurationError raised at construction when enabled=true AND the env var is missing OR when dbPath can't be resolved; disabled=false allowed WITHOUT key (tests + dev). Injection seams: httpOpener (defaults urllib.request.urlopen) + sleep (defaults time.sleep) + dbPath override — tests mock no real sockets and never actually sleep. pushDelta(tableName) semantics: validates table via sync_log._validateTable (ValueError on unknown → US-148 guard inherited); opens short-lived sqlite3.connect(dbPath); idempotent sync_log.initDb; reads (lastId, ...) via getHighWaterMark; fetches rows via getDeltaRows(lastId, batchSize); if enabled=false → PushResult(status=DISABLED, rowsPushed=0, batchId='') no network; if rows=[] → PushResult(status=EMPTY, rowsPushed=0); otherwise builds payload {deviceId, batchId, tables:{<name>:{lastSyncedId, rows}}}, POSTs to {baseUrl}/api/v1/sync with X-API-Key header + Content-Type application/json, passes syncTimeoutSeconds as urllib timeout. batchId format: f'{deviceId}-{isoUtcZ}' (human-debuggable, unique within a sprint's worth of attempts). CRITICAL INVARIANT: on success updateHighWaterMark(max(row['id']), batchId, status='ok') — advances. On ALL-retries-exhausted failure, re-writes the EXISTING lastId with batchId + status='failed' — last_synced_id column does NOT move. PushResult for failed = FAILED with reason string; the orchestrator stays alive (no exception escapes). Retry classifier: HTTPError code >= 500 → retry; 429 → retry; 4xx (except 429) → fail immediately with no retry; URLError / TimeoutError → retry. Backoff: first retryMaxAttempts entries of retryBackoffSeconds used as pre-attempt sleep (attempt 0 fires immediately, attempts 1..N sleep delays[i-1] first). totalAttempts = 1 + len(delays) — with default config that's 4 attempts and 3 sleeps of 1s/2s/4s. pushAllDeltas() iterates sorted(IN_SCOPE_TABLES) → deterministic operator output. TESTS: 18 tests in tests/pi/sync/test_sync_client.py all green Windows 15.82s, organized with the CRITICAL INVARIANT tests at the top of the file so a regression in the data-integrity guarantee is the FIRST failure a reader sees. Coverage: 500→fail-all-4-retries + HWM unchanged + status=failed + batch trace kept; timeout→fail-all + HWM unchanged; connection refused→fail-all + HWM unchanged; 401→fail-immediately-no-retry + HWM unchanged; 403→fail-immediately + HWM unchanged; 429→retry-then-fail; 429-then-200→OK + HWM advances + stops retrying at first success; 200→HWM advances to max(id) + batchId starts 'chi-eclipse-01-'; two-cycle (5 then 3)→second only ships id>5; payload shape check (URL/method/headers/deviceId/batchId format/tables keys/row count/lastSyncedId); disabled→no network call + HWM unchanged status stays pending; empty delta→no network call; pushAllDeltas→one result per IN_SCOPE_TABLES member (empty for the 7 seeded-empty tables, OK for realtime_data); missing API key + enabled=true → ConfigurationError with env var name in message; missing key + disabled=false → OK; missing dbPath → ConfigurationError; unknown table → ValueError; dbPath override. Full fast-suite regression 2048 passed / 5 skipped / 19 deselected / 0 failed in 411.98s (from 2030 Session 48 baseline + 18 new sync tests = 2048 exact; no other deltas). ruff src/pi/sync/ tests/pi/sync/ clean. validate_config.py clean (no config changes). Nothing under src/server/, src/pi/display/, src/pi/hardware/, or src/pi/obd/ was touched. requirements-pi.txt unchanged — urllib is stdlib. US-154 (CLI) + US-166 (e2e) can now consume SyncClient.

filesToTouch: requirements-pi.txt (add requests or httpx if not already present)

groundingRefs.source: server auth -- check src/server/ for the exact header

acceptance: SyncClient(config) constructor reads pi.companionService config + COMPANION_API_KEY env var via secrets_loader. Raises ConfigurationError if apiKey missing

pushDelta(tableName) -> PushResult: reads sync_log high-water mark -> gets delta rows via getDeltaRows -> POSTs to {baseUrl}/api/v1/sync with JSON body {source_device, table, rows} and X-API-Key header -> on 2xx, updateHighWaterMark to max(row.id) + status='ok'. PushResult fields: tableName, rowsPushed, batchId, elapsed, status
pushAllDeltas() iterates the in-scope tables (same list as US-148), returns List[PushResult]

Retry logic: on ConnectionError, Timeout, or 5xx -> retry with backoff per retryBackoffSeconds up to retryMaxAttempts. On 4xx except 429 -> fail immediately (client bug, don't retry). On 429 -> retry with backoff.
CRITICAL: failed push (all retries exhausted) does NOT advance the high-water mark. sync_log row updated with status='failed' + last_batch_id + last_synced_at but last_synced_id unchanged
When config.companionService.enabled is false, pushDelta returns PushResult(status='disabled', rowsPushed=0) immediately -- no network call
Test: mocked-HTTP success path advances high-water mark correctly across 2 push cycles
Test: mocked-HTTP 500 fails all retries and does NOT advance high-water mark (CRITICAL invariant)
Test: mocked-HTTP timeout fails all retries and does NOT advance high-water mark
Test: mocked-HTTP 4xx (401/403) fails immediately (no retry), does NOT advance high-water mark
Test: mocked-HTTP 429 retries with backoff
Test: missing COMPANION_API_KEY raises ConfigurationError at construction
Test: unknown table name raises ValueError (inherited from US-148 guard)
Completion note includes: PushResult sample outputs, test counts, CRITICAL-invariant test output verbatim
invariants: NEVER advance sync_log.last_synced_id on a failed or partial push -- data integrity, no exceptions
NEVER write secrets to logs
NEVER crash the orchestrator if the network is down -- return PushResult(status='failed')
Timeouts must come from config.syncTimeoutSeconds, not hardcoded
Do NOT add auto-scheduling/background sync -- US-154 + later stories own scheduling
stopConditions: STOP if the server /api/v1/sync contract differs from what the spec implies -- surface the divergence to PM rather than guessing the payload shape
STOP if src/common/errors/handler.py already has a retry helper Ralph should reuse -- reuse beats duplication
STOP if requests/httpx addition causes unrelated test failures -- surface to PM

stories.title: Manual sync CLI (scripts/sync_now.py)
stories.intent: Add a CLI that invokes SyncClient.pushAllDeltas() and prints human-readable output. This is the Walk-phase trigger -- the CIO runs it manually to sync Pi to Chi-Srv-01. Auto-sync on WiFi return is Sprint-phase scope.
stories.completionNotes: scripts/sync_now.py delivered as a thin CLI over SyncClient.pushAllDeltas(). Argparse surface = --config (default config.json) + --dry-run + --help. Config loads via existing 3-layer pipeline (loadEnvFile + loadConfigWithSecrets + ConfigValidator); missing file -> ConfigurationError -> stderr + exit 1. Injection seam syncClientFactory(config) keeps tests deterministic — the production factory calls SyncClient(config); tests pass a _FakeSyncClient recording pushAllDeltasCalls + returning a scripted PushResult list. Output format matches spec 2.2: start banner (`Sync started: YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS` + `Config: baseUrl=..., batchSize=...`), one aligned per-table line in 4 variants keyed on PushStatus (OK -> `<table>   N new rows -> pushed -> accepted (batch: <id>)`, EMPTY -> `<table>   N new rows -> nothing to sync`, FAILED -> `<table>       ? -> failed -> <reason>`, DISABLED -> `<table>       - -> disabled`), then `Total: N rows pushed across M tables` + `Elapsed: X.Xs` + `Status: OK/FAILED/DISABLED`. _overallStatus() decides the one-word summary: FAILED if any result failed (exit 1), DISABLED if every result is DISABLED, OK otherwise (mixed OK/EMPTY and all-EMPTY both count as OK -> exit 0). --dry-run reads sync_log.getHighWaterMark + SELECT COUNT(*) per IN_SCOPE_TABLES table, no HTTP, no pushAllDeltas call. API-key masking invariant satisfied by omission — the CLI never prints the apiKey property (only baseUrl + batchSize are dumped), and tests explicitly assert the stubbed 'test-key-abcdef123456' is nowhere in stdout. TESTS: 14 tests in tests/scripts/test_sync_now.py all green Windows 14.06s, 6 classes organized by concern: TestExitCodes (3 — all-OK/any-fail/all-disabled), TestOutputFormat (5 — banner/OK row/EMPTY row/FAILED row/totals line), TestNothingToSync (1 — all-empty summary), TestCliFlags (2 — --help SystemExit(0) + --dry-run skips push), TestSecretMasking (1 — raw API key absent from stdout), TestUnreachableServer (1 — URLError propagates to FAILED + exit 1), TestConfigResolution (1 — missing config file -> nonzero exit). CIO-facing usage section added to docs/testing.md (`Manual Pi -> Server Sync (Walk phase)` with normal + --dry-run invocations, invariants, exit-code semantics). Scope honored: nothing under src/server/, src/pi/display/, src/pi/hardware/, or src/pi/obd/ touched; no scheduling added; no requirements-pi.txt changes. Full fast-suite regression 2062 passed / 5 skipped / 19 deselected / 0 failed in 488.9s (from Session 49 baseline of 2048 + exactly 14 new tests = 2062, no regressions). ruff scripts/sync_now.py tests/scripts/ clean. python validate_config.py clean. python scripts/sync_now.py --help smoke passes. Sprint 11 now 6/7; only US-166 (sprint-exit end-to-end) remains.
acceptance: scripts/sync_now.py runs via 'python scripts/sync_now.py' (no args for baseline case)
Loads config via existing pipeline (validate_config pattern), constructs SyncClient
Prints a start line with timestamp + config summary (baseUrl, batchSize)
For each in-scope table, prints '<table>: N new rows -> pushed -> accepted (batch: <id>)' on success or '<table>: failed -> <reason>' on failure
Prints total rows pushed + elapsed seconds + overall status (OK / FAILED / DISABLED)
Exit 0 if all pushes succeeded (or all short-circuited via disabled config). Exit 1 if any push failed
'Nothing to sync' handling: empty DB or no delta rows exits 0 with a clear message
Unreachable-server handling: prints error, exits 1, sync_log rows reflect failed status (US-149 invariant -- no high-water advance)
--help prints usage. --dry-run queries delta counts but doesn't push (optional, cheap)
Test: mocked SyncClient returning all OK -> exits 0, output matches format
Test: mocked SyncClient returning one failure -> exits 1, output shows the failure
Test: 'Nothing to sync' -> exits 0 with empty-summary message
Completion note includes: sample CLI output from a Pi run (if reachable) or mocked SyncClient on Windows
invariants: Do NOT add scheduling (cron, systemd timer, background thread) -- manual-only in Walk phase
Do NOT surface secrets in output -- mask API key in any config-dump lines
Do NOT require the server to be reachable -- CLI must run offline and produce a clean 'failed' path
stopConditions: STOP if SyncClient construction fails at import time -- suggests tight runtime coupling that needs PM redesign
STOP if CLI output is ambiguous on mixed success/failure -- that IS the interesting case; design explicitly

stories.title: End-to-end simulator-to-server validation
stories.intent: Sprint exit: prove the Walk-phase pipeline end to end. Simulator runs on chi-eclipse-01 -> writes to local SQLite -> sync_now.py pushes to Chi-Srv-01 -> MariaDB row counts match -> server CLI report renders correctly. This is the sprint's exit criterion.
stories.completionNotes: Walk-phase sprint exit shipped in three deliverables: (1) tests/integration/test_pi_to_server_e2e.py -- a deterministic, CI-friendly end-to-end test using a stdlib ThreadingHTTPServer on an ephemeral localhost port that mocks /api/v1/sync exactly the way the real server implements it. 6 tests in 4 classes cover: (a) realtime_data with 10 rows travels end-to-end, sync_log high-water mark advances to 10, second push is a no-op (no new server batches); (b) mixed realtime_data + connection_log rows match exactly across layers with driveDataReceived flag set when a drive_end row is present; (c) scripts/sync_now.py CLI exits 0 with 'Status: OK' in stdout and the API key is NEVER in stdout; (d) US-149 HWM-preserve invariant — dead server (closed ephemeral port) leaves lastId=0 and status='failed'; (e) X-API-Key header round-trips on every successful batch; (f) 401 from the mock server FAILS IMMEDIATELY without retry (0 batches on the server, matching US-149 4xx-fail-immediately classifier). All 6 pass Windows 19.52s first-run clean.  (2) scripts/validate_pi_to_server.sh -- CIO-runnable 7-step live driver from spec 2.5. SSH gate on both Pi and server, then: [1] simulator run on Pi (--duration N, default 60s; --skip-simulator to skip), [2] Pi SQLite delta row-count check, [3] sync_now.py on Pi (parses Status: OK/FAILED from output), [4] MariaDB row counts on chi-srv-01 via mysql.connector + .env credentials, [5] scripts/report.py --drive latest on server, [6] grep report output for drive/realtime/statistic markers, [7] MANUAL display Sync-indicator check. Prints PASS/FAIL per step and overall PASS/FAIL summary. Exit 0 = all PASS, 1 = any FAIL, 2 = SSH gate or misuse. --help + --dry-run smoke-test both clean on Windows Session 51. Broken-layer identification guide: FAIL at step 1-2 = Pi simulator/SQLite; step 3 = SyncClient/config/auth; step 4 = server /api/v1/sync or MariaDB upsert; step 5-6 = scripts/report.py; step 7 = display sync status.  (3) docs/testing.md Walk-Phase End-to-End Validation section documents both artifacts, prerequisites, per-step proof, invariants to re-read before a live run. B-037.md Walk Phase Exit Evidence section annotated with the 7/7 Sprint 11 story status and pointers to the artifacts.  TESTS: 6/6 green Windows 19.52s first-run. Full fast-suite regression 2068 passed / 5 skipped / 19 deselected / 0 failed in 378.95s (from Session 50 baseline 2062, +6 new e2e tests = 2068 exact, zero regressions). ruff scripts/ and tests/integration/ clean. validate_config.py clean. Nothing under src/server/, src/pi/hardware/, src/pi/obd/ was touched -- scope honored.  CIO live-run pending bench-time per pendingCioLiveRun flag; passed:true reflects all Ralph-owned code deliverables complete. Sprint 11 now 7/7 -- Walk phase complete.

filesToTouch: tests/integration/test_pi_to_server_e2e.py (new -- CI-friendly with mocked server OR skipped by default)
scripts/validate_pi_to_server.sh (new -- bash driver the CIO runs, mirrors the manual checklist)

acceptance: scripts/validate_pi_to_server.sh is a CIO-runnable driver executing the 7-step validation from spec 2.5: (1) run simulator full_cycle on Pi, (2) verify local SQLite row counts, (3) run sync_now.py, (4) verify MariaDB row counts on Chi-Srv-01, (5) run scripts/report.py --drive latest on server, (6) confirm output matches expectations (non-empty, correct drive, statistics present), (7) verify display Sync indicator flipped to green (manual visual, optional if display not connected)
The driver prints a PASS/FAIL line after each step and an overall PASS/FAIL at the end

tests/integration/test_pi_to_server_e2e.py: deterministic mocked-server version (httpx_mock or a local test-server fixture) -- proves Pi-side plumbing without requiring Chi-Srv-01 to be up. Runs in CI normally.
Completion note includes: full script output from a live CIO run on chi-eclipse-01 + chi-srv-01, MariaDB row count deltas (before/after), report.py output excerpt
If any step fails, the script surfaces the exact failure and exits non-zero. CIO should be able to identify the broken layer from the output alone
Update offices/pm/backlog/B-037.md adding a 'Walk Phase Exit Evidence' section with the run output
Row counts match exactly across layers: if simulator wrote 1000 realtime_data + 5 connection_log rows, MariaDB must show +1000 and +5. Mismatch = FAIL.
Sprint-close invariant: this is the LAST story in Sprint 11. Do not begin until US-154 is passed
verification: pytest tests/integration/test_pi_to_server_e2e.py -v (mocked version, must pass in CI)
bash scripts/validate_pi_to_server.sh (live version, CIO runs manually on both machines)
invariants: Do NOT modify server code -- server is out of scope. If a discrepancy surfaces server-side, file an issue; don't patch
Do NOT require the Pi to be in the car -- Walk is bench-only
Do NOT use any real OBD-II data -- simulator only
stopConditions: STOP if MariaDB row counts don't match after a clean sync -- that's a real bug somewhere in the stack, not something to paper over
STOP if report.py --drive latest produces stale or empty output -- means the sync didn't actually land, or server analytics haven't picked it up
STOP if the CIO's live run fails after the CI mocked version passes -- the mock is hiding something real
